§6Plutonium Hexafluoride§f

§aThe gaseous intermediary for plutonium enrichment — turn raw plutonium into separated isotopes!§f

§bPlutonium Hexafluoride§f (PuF6) is a corrosive radioactive gas that serves as the intermediary step in plutonium enrichment. Unlike uranium, plutonium requires no additional processing to become a hexafluoride.

§6Production§f (Chemical Plant, 10s, 500HE/t):
§b• §e1,000mB§f Water
§b• §bPlutonium Powder§f
§b• §e3×§f Fluorite
→ §e900mB§f §bPuF6§f

§6Storage§f:
§b• Requires§f a §bspecialized tank§f
§b• Stored in§f Fluid Barrels

§6Processing§f:
§b• Gas Centrifuge§f: enriches PuF6 to separate §bPu-238§f from §bReactor Grade Plutonium§f
§b• SILEX§f: can further separate Reactor Grade Plutonium into §bPu-239§f and §bPu-240§f (skips a step)

§c⚠ Safety§f: PuF6 is §bcorrosive§f, §bradioactive§f, and §bgaseous§f. Always wear full PPE!

§eTip§f: The §bSILEX§f is more efficient than the Gas Centrifuge for plutonium enrichment. It can process PuF6 directly and separate RGP into pure Pu-239 and Pu-240 in one step!
